title: Air-fryer garlic and thyme mushrooms
subtitle: Juicy mushrooms with golden edges in 10 minutes
excerpt: Chestnut mushrooms tossed with olive oil, crushed garlic, and thyme, cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 10 minutes. The air fryer concentrates the mushroom flavour by evaporating surface moisture quickly, producing a deeply savoury result that takes twice as long on a stovetop.
sourceNotes: Mushrooms on toast, mushrooms alongside a full English, and mushrooms as a side for steak are all long-established British eating occasions. Chestnut mushrooms have a firmer texture and earthier flavour than white button mushrooms and hold their shape better during high-heat cooking. The air fryer evaporates the mushrooms' considerable water content faster than a frying pan, producing a more concentrated, golden result.

content.text: Do not crowd the mushrooms. Mushrooms release large amounts of water during cooking. If they are stacked or touching, they steam each other and turn grey and watery rather than developing golden edges. A single layer with space between each mushroom allows the released moisture to evaporate immediately rather than accumulating in the basket.

items.prepNote: halved if large, left whole if small

content.text: Wipe the {{chestnut-mushrooms}} clean with a damp cloth; do not wash under water. Toss with {{olive-oil}}, {{garlic}}, {{thyme}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}}. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 2 minutes. Spread the mushrooms in a single layer in the basket, cut-side down where possible. Cook at 200°C for 10 minutes, shaking the basket once at the 5-minute mark, until golden on the cut faces and the edges have browned slightly. Remove and scatter with {{parsley-flat}} if using. Serve immediately.

items.symptom: Mushrooms grey and watery rather than golden
items.cause: Basket overcrowded, or mushrooms washed under running water before cooking
items.fix: Only wipe mushrooms clean: washing adds water they will release during cooking. Give every mushroom its own space in the basket. Cook in two batches if needed

items.cause: Crushed garlic in direct contact with the hot basket
items.fix: Toss the garlic through the mushrooms so it is carried rather than sitting on the bare metal. Or add the garlic only in the last 2 minutes of cooking

items.symptom: Mushrooms shrinking to half their size
items.cause: Normal: mushrooms lose most of their weight as water evaporates during cooking
items.fix: This is expected. A 400g batch of raw mushrooms will reduce to about 200g after cooking. Account for this when deciding on quantities

content.text: Air-fryer garlic mushrooms are a fast side dish and a breakfast component. Serve on toasted sourdough with a poached egg, alongside a steak as a sauce-free accompaniment, or as part of a full English breakfast alongside bacon and sausages. They cook in less time than it takes to fry mushrooms properly on the hob.
